articles.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
articles.summary: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

articles.title: The President Is Coming to the White House Correspondent’s Dinner
articles.summary: A president who relishes attacking the news media is set to break his boycott of an event celebrating the news media. What could go wrong?

articles.title: Vance Heads to New Talks With Iran. At Stake: Peace and His Own Standing.
articles.summary: The vice president is again center stage, after abruptly leaving the first round of high-level Iranian peace talks without an agreement.

articles.title: Trump Administration to Begin Refunding $166 Billion in Tariffs
articles.summary: The government will debut a system to repay importers two months after the Supreme Court struck down tariffs at the heart of the president’s trade policy.

articles.title: Why Gas Prices Go Up Fast and Take So Long to Fall
articles.summary: Fuel station owners take some of the hit for consumers when oil prices surge. On the way down, they try to get their money back.

articles.title: Data center disputes have been local. But the midterms might change that
articles.summary: Anger over the data center boom has spilled into politics with voters unseating local politicians who support them. It's become an issue hard to ignore in the midterm elections.

articles.title: Voters say they feel confused and misled on Virginia's redistricting vote
articles.summary: Contradictory election mailers, conflicting TV ads and vague wording on the ballot have Virginia voters saying that the campaigns on either side of the redistricting vote are muddying the waters.

articles.title: Spanish speakers learn strategies to pass English-only driving test in Florida
articles.summary: A new English-only driving test rule in Florida is fueling a surge in strategy lessons for Spanish speakers where they learn to figure out the questions without having to take English language courses

articles.title: The Sonoran Desert teems with wildlife. These 3D scans could help protect its future
articles.summary: A new art exhibit in Phoenix features some of the world's prickliest plants. It could also help save them.

articles.title: Who owns presidential records? Trump's Justice Department says it's him
articles.summary: The Trump administration asserts a nearly 50-year-old law requiring the preservation of presidential records is unconstitutional. Historians warn important papers could be destroyed.

articles.title: Got wearable data? Your doctor can help you connect the dots
articles.summary: The American Academy of Neurology issued guidance on using wearable data devices, like smart watches or Oura rings, to track key health metrics that can help flag serious conditions.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Trump announces planned Iran war peace talks, Tehran signals it may boycott negotiations amid ongoing U.S. naval pressure, businesses can now apply for Trump tariff refunds.

articles.title: After massive cuts, U.S. says HIV work aboard is going well, but experts disagree
articles.summary: The Trump administration has released new data on the country's HIV/AIDS work abroad. It is claiming the numbers are good news, but many HIV experts say they paint a worrisome picture.

articles.title: Ukraine's fast-growing defense tech sector takes center stage in Iran war
articles.summary: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.

articles.title: Why Your Paycheck Feels Smaller
articles.summary: Ben Casselman, our chief economics correspondent, explains why wages are not keeping up with inflation and what that means for American workers and the economy.

articles.title: The Iran War Sent Shock Waves Through Asia That Are Likely to Spread
articles.summary: The Asia-Pacific was hit hard and quick by the war in Iran and its energy bottlenecks. Scenes of crisis there indicate that problems are multiplying and spreading.

articles.title: U.K police investigating if attacks in London are the work of Iranian proxies
articles.summary: U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.

articles.title: From Pulpit to Pews, Trump and Pope Are on the Minds of Catholics
articles.summary: At churches with connections to Pope Leo and the Trump administration, pastors and parishioners speak out on the feud between the president and the pontiff.

articles.title: U.S. says it seized an Iranian cargo ship, ceasefire expires soon
articles.summary: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.

articles.title: Energy Secretary Says Gas Prices May Stay Above $3 Until 2027
articles.summary: Secretary of Energy Chris Wright’s acknowledgment in a TV interview undercut President Trump’s earlier claim that price increases would be “short-term.”

articles.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

articles.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
articles.summary: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

articles.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
articles.summary: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
